$comment: FAMILY HUB SAFETY POLICY. The harness implements the rule KINDS; this file says
which of THIS product's calls they apply to, and with what vocabulary. Everything
here used to be hardcoded inside SafetyFilter, which is why the generic core knew
grades.overrides may only make a grade STRICTER than the [SideEffect] attribute on
the method (A0 < A1 < A2 < AX). Loosening one throws at startup — see the ratchet in
SafetyPolicy.GradeFor. That is why this file can be edited without fear: the worst a
mistake here can do is demand MORE consent than the code intends.
Rules read constraints.hard and nothing else. Soft preferences never gate.

rules.$comment: Allergens/diet/medical. No module binding: a forbidden ingredient is forbidden wherever it appears.
Screen recipe RESULTS too, so a forbidden dish never reaches the model as a candidate. Recipes tag themselves with the same vocabulary (contains: [dairy, gluten]).
The budget guardrail: only the call that actually spends money.
Quiet hours: things that make noise in the house.
